title: CPRIT Established - First Grants Awarded
description: Voters approved Prop 15 authorizing $3B for cancer research. BCM and MD Anderson liver researchers were early recipients, launching Texas's HCC infrastructure.

title: Hepatocellular Carcinoma
institution: Baylor College of Medicine / Houston VA
description: Landmark global HCC review documenting the rapid rise of HCV-related HCC as the fastest-growing cause of US cancer deaths.

title: HCC Incidence & Risk Factors in Texas Latinos
description: First study documenting South Texas Latinos have the nation's highest HCC rates (10.6/100K). Identified obesity and diabetes as key modifiable risk factors.

title: Epidemiology of Viral Hepatitis and HCC
description: Quantified population-attributable fraction of HBV/HCV in HCC; foundational for Texas public health interventions.

title: HCC in Texas Latinos 1995-2010: An Update
description: Confirmed TX Latino male/female HCC rates 3.1x and 4.0x higher than non-Hispanic Whites.

title: Texas HCC Consortium (THCCC)
institution: BCM / UTSW / MD Anderson / UT San Antonio
description: CPRIT Multi-Investigator Award launching the THCCC - 5000-patient prospective cohort at 5 Texas centers for HCC risk factors and early detection biomarkers.

title: Lifestyle & Clinical Correlates of HCC - Aflatoxin Study
description: First U.S. study linking aflatoxin exposure to HCC in a Latino-majority population. Cases had 6x higher odds of detectable aflatoxins in blood.

title: AFP + Ultrasound for HCC Detection: Meta-Analysis
authors: Tzartzeva K, Obi J, Rich NE, Singal AG et al.
description: 32 studies: AFP + ultrasound improves HCC sensitivity to 63% vs 45% for ultrasound alone. Formed basis for updated AASLD surveillance guidelines.

title: THCCC Cohort - 2000+ Patients Enrolled
institution: BCM / UTSW / MD Anderson / UT San Antonio
description: Semi-annual ultrasound, AFP, labs, and biospecimens collected across 5 Texas sites. One of the largest prospective HCC biomarker repositories in the US.

title: Epidemiology and Management of HCC
authors: Singal AG; El-Serag HB et al.
description: Comprehensive review of HCC risk stratification, surveillance, staging, and treatment. Addressed NAFLD/NASH-HCC epidemic and rising rates in Hispanic populations.

title: TeCH Center CAP Round 1
description: CPRIT Collaborative Award establishing the Texas Collaborative Center for HCC (TeCH), coordinating statewide HCC research.

title: HCC Biomarker Validation - El-Serag
description: Biomarker validation grant for HCC early detection research within the THCCC cohort.

title: Patient-Centered Liver Cancer Prevention - Hwang
description: Patient-centered liver cancer prevention initiative targeting at-risk populations at MD Anderson.

title: Texas HCC Consortium (THCCC) Cohort Study Design
authors: Loo N; El-Serag HB; Singal AG et al.
institution: BCM / UTSW / MD Anderson / UT San Antonio
description: Describes the THCCC 5000-patient prospective cohort design and multi-center data collection protocol.

title: Metformin Modifies HCC Disparity in Diabetic Men
authors: Wang CP; Ramirez AG et al.
description: In 84433 men with T2D, metformin was associated with reduced HCC risk with stronger effects in Hispanic and African American men.

title: Cirrhosis Cohort Biomarker Surveillance - Singal
description: 3000-patient cirrhosis cohort developing biomarker-based HCC surveillance algorithms.

title: Genetic Epidemiology of HCC in Hispanics - Thrift
description: Genetic epidemiology of HCC in Hispanics; focused on PNPLA3 and TM6SF2 variants.

title: Epidemiology & Surveillance for HCC: New Trends
description: Documents shift from viral hepatitis to NAFLD/NASH as dominant HCC etiology; projects 122% increase in NAFLD-HCC by 2030.

title: AGA Update: HCC Surveillance in NAFLD
description: Joint AGA expert review on HCC screening in the rapidly growing NAFLD population. Addresses surveillance intervals and novel biomarkers.

title: Community HCC Prevention at Safety-Net Clinics
description: Community-based HCC prevention initiative at safety-net clinics targeting underserved Texas populations.

title: GALAD + AFP-L3 Biomarker Validation - El-Serag
description: Validation of GALAD score and AFP-L3 as early detection biomarkers within the THCCC cohort.

title: TeCH Center CAP Round 2
description: TeCH Center renewal expanding NASH-HCC research and health equity initiatives across Texas institutions.

title: HCC Surveillance Improves Outcomes: Meta-Analysis
description: 59 studies / 145396 patients: Surveillance yields 1.86x early-stage detection and 33% mortality reduction (HR 0.67).

title: The Texas Collaborative Center for HCC (TeCH)
authors: Harrison AC; El-Serag HB et al.
description: Describes TeCH's multi-institutional research, education, and advocacy framework targeting reduction of Texas liver cancer mortality by 2030.

title: Predictive Biomarkers for Pediatric HCC - Lopez-Terrada
description: Predictive biomarkers for pediatric HCC and hepatoblastoma at Texas Children's Hospital.

title: Molecular Mechanisms of HCC Progression - Zhong
description: Investigation of molecular mechanisms driving HCC progression and therapeutic targets.

title: Prevention of HCC - White Paper
description: Consensus recommendations covering HBV vaccination, HCV cure, metabolic intervention, and health equity strategies for Hispanic Texas communities.

title: HES v2.0 Outperforms GALAD for HCC Detection
authors: El-Serag HB; Jin M et al.
institution: Baylor College of Medicine / THCCC Network
description: Phase 3 biomarker evaluation: HES v2.0 (AFP + AFP-L3 + DCP + clinical variables) demonstrated superior sensitivity and specificity over GALAD for early HCC detection.

title: CPRIT Surpasses $4B + NCI SPORE Designation
description: CPRIT surpasses $4B total (~$59-77M to liver cancer). UT Southwestern receives the first Texas NCI SPORE for Liver Cancer with Singal, Yopp et al.
